target.title: Expose chat.inject metadata for plugin approval updates
target.body:
## Review context Concrete plugin: [Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit](https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit). The intent is to keep World ID / AgentKit out of OpenClaw core while letting external HITL plugins use the existing approval flow end-to-end. This PR's role: durable chat/status delivery with metadata and idempotency so external approval cards survive history/live events. Bundle: - [#82431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82431) exposes approval action metadata/UI rendering. - [#82434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82434) adds verified approval resolution with plugin ownership checks. - [#82471](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82471) adds chat/status metadata and idempotency for durable approval updates. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant Gateway participant Plugin as HITL plugin participant UI as TUI/Web UI participant Verifier as External verifier Agent->>Gateway: protected tool call Gateway->>Plugin: approval hook/request Gateway->>UI: approval card + plugin actions UI->>Plugin: user chooses verify once / trust session / deny Plugin->>Verifier: external proof flow Verifier-->>Plugin: verified result Plugin->>Gateway: resolve verified approval Gateway->>Agent: allow/deny blocked tool Plugin->>UI: status/update messages with idempotency ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Approval action surface
#82431"] --> B["Verified resolver
#82434"] B --> C["Status/chat idempotency
#82471"] C --> D["Usable external HITL plugin flow"] ``` Trust boundary to review: should a plugin that already owns a pending approval be allowed to complete that specific approval after external verification, through a typed SDK/Gateway path with admin scope and ownership checks? ## Summary - Let operator-admin `chat.inject` accept optional `idempotencyKey`, `command`, `interactive`, and `channelData` fields. - Persist and broadcast those fields on injected assistant transcript messages so plugin approval/status cards survive `chat.history` and live `chat` events. - Enforce `idempotencyKey` replay at the serialized transcript append boundary so concurrent retries do not append or broadcast duplicate approval cards. - Expose a narrow `injectChatMessageOverGateway` helper through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime` so external plugins do not need a generic admin Gateway client. - Refresh the generated protocol/API surfaces, document the metadata contract, and add live loopback Gateway proof for WebSocket broadcast plus `chat.history` persistence. ## Verification - `pnpm protocol:check` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` - `pnpm docs:list` - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-transcript-inject.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/operator-chat-client.ts src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts src/gateway/gateway.test.ts docs/gateway/protocol.md docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/gateway.test.ts --testNamePattern "preserves chat.inject metadata through live ws broadcast and chat.history" --testTimeout 120000 --reporter verbose` - `git diff --check` ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: `chat.inject` can carry AgentKit-style approval/status metadata (`idempotencyKey`, `command`, `interactive`, `channelData`) through Gateway validation, transcript append, live WebSocket `chat` broadcast, and `chat.history`, while same-key retries dedupe before any second append or live broadcast. External plugins can use the new narrow SDK helper instead of a generic admin Gateway client. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS on May 16, 2026, with a real loopback Gateway server started from the test harness, token auth enabled, one admin WebSocket client issuing RPCs, and one separate watcher WebSocket client receiving live events. Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the loopback Gateway, created a dashboard session through `sessions.create`, sent `chat.inject` with AgentKit-shaped `interactive` and `channelData`, replayed the same request with the same `idempotencyKey`, asserted the second response was `{ ok: true, deduped: true }`, asserted the watcher saw exactly one live `chat` event carrying the metadata, queried `chat.history`, and asserted the persisted history message carried the same metadata. Evidence after fix: ```text $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/gateway.test.ts --testNamePattern "preserves chat.inject metadata through live ws broadcast and chat.history" --testTimeout 120000 --reporter verbose ✓ |gateway-client| ../../src/gateway/gateway.test.ts > gateway e2e > preserves chat.inject metadata through live ws broadcast and chat.history 887ms Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 1 passed | 4 skipped (5) ``` Additional focused proof: ```text $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot Test Files 4 passed (4) Tests 16 passed (16) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot Test Files 9 passed (9) Tests 271 passed (271) $ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke (exit 0) $ node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/gateway.test.ts All matched files use the correct format. $ git diff --check (no output) ``` Observed result after fix: live Gateway behavior now demonstrates the end-to-end host seam this PR owns: metadata-bearing plugin approval/status cards are accepted over the admin RPC, delivered once over WebSocket, durable through `chat.history`, and safely idempotent on retry. The public plugin SDK exposes a focused `injectChatMessageOverGateway` helper for external ClawHub plugins to call this path. What was not tested: full release suite and live World ID credential verification against production AgentKit services; this PR verifies the generic OpenClaw host API contract that the external ClawHub plugin needs. The full AgentKit HITL stack also depends on #82431 for plugin approval action/no-route pending metadata and #82434 for plugin-owned verified approval resolution. Refs #82336. ## External plugin integration proof Behavior addressed: the external ClawHub AgentKit plugin can consume the combined temporary OpenClaw host API branch that includes this PR's durable `chat.inject` metadata and narrow chat injection helper. Real environment tested: GitHub Actions in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit` on May 16, 2026, checking out `Guardiola31337/openclaw@agentkit/external-plugin-host-apis` and linking it into the external plugin package. Exact steps or command run after this patch: the remote workflow checked out the temporary OpenClaw host branch, installed with frozen lockfiles, ran `pnpm build gatewayWatch`, ran `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, linked the host checkout into the AgentKit plugin, then ran `pnpm test:hitl` and `pnpm pack:check`. Evidence after fix: ```text $ gh run watch 25972624778 --repo Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit --exit-status ✓ main CI · 25972624778 ✓ build in 1m46s ✓ Run pnpm build gatewayWatch ✓ Run pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke ✓ Run pnpm dev:link-openclaw .openclaw-source ✓ Run pnpm test:hitl ✓ Run pnpm pack:check ``` Observed result after fix: the external plugin builds against the split OpenClaw host APIs, uses the narrow `injectChatMessageOverGateway` helper instead of a generic admin client, the AgentKit HITL proof passes, and the package dry-run includes the expected ClawHub plugin artifact. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification. This remote proof exercises the integrated temporary host branch because the external plugin requires this PR together with #82431 and #82434 for the full HITL path.

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI could restore a persisted historical routed session key, such as direct, group, dm, per-peer, or threaded routed session forms, after a reload.
- Why it matters: reopening Control UI could land the user in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI appear misrouted while the backend remained pointed at main.
- Intended outcome: settings restore sanitizes historical routed session keys and falls back to main before the restored session state is used.
- Out of scope: this does not change gateway routing, heartbeat targeting, live session switching, or backend session semantics.
- Reviewer focus: the routed-session key predicate in ui/src/ui/storage.ts and the storage restore regression cases in ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts.

## Linked context

Closes #61661

Related PRs or discussions: none beyond the linked issue and this PR's ClawSweeper review history.

Maintainer or owner request: not a direct maintainer request; this is a focused bug fix for the linked routed-session restore issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI persisted settings could restore historical routed direct/group/dm/per-peer/threaded session keys instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of openclaw/openclaw on Linux against the PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
  - git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts covers these after-fix restore outcomes:
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded per-peer routed session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a legacy threaded dm session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account-channel-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session with thread suffix
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account routed group session with topic suffix

  Latest current-head validation reported on the PR:
  - storage restore test file passed: 33 tests
  - check:test-types passed
  - targeted agentic files from the failed CI job passed: 212 tests
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD passed
  - git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main passed
- Observed result after fix: persisted historical routed session selections are normalized to main during Control UI settings restore; non-historical agent main session keys remain valid.
- What was not tested: browser screenshot/video proof and live backend routing, because the fix is limited to the local settings restore boundary and does not change runtime routing.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof is from local branch validation and copied terminal output, not a full browser session recording.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-fix storage restore trusted persisted routed session keys and had no restore regression coverage for these historical routed forms.

## Tests and validation

Commands run for this body repair and live-state validation:

- git fetch upstream --prune
- git fetch origin --prune
- git merge-tree --write-tree origin/fix/control-ui-routed-session-restore upstream/main
- authenticated GitHub API read of PR #78741 and current base-branch .github/pull_request_template.md
- authenticated GitHub API update of PR #78741 body
- authenticated GitHub API re-read of PR #78741 body headings

Current-head branch validation already captured for the code change:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
- git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
- git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main

Regression coverage added or updated: ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts now covers routed direct, routed group, per-peer direct, threaded per-peer, threaded direct, legacy threaded dm, per-account direct, group thread suffix, and per-account group topic suffix restore cases.

What failed before this fix, if known: historical routed session keys could be restored from persisted Control UI settings instead of being discarded back to main.

If no test was added, why not: not applicable; focused storage restore tests were added and expanded.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes/No)

Yes. Reloaded Control UI sessions now fall back to main when the persisted value is a historical routed session key.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (Yes/No)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (Yes/No)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The key-shape classifier could accidentally treat a valid persisted session key as historical.

How is that risk mitigated?

The predicate is scoped to routed direct/group/dm/thread forms, and regression tests cover both sanitized historical shapes and valid non-historical agent main session keys.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper and maintainers can re-check the PR body against the current upstream template and continue review of the existing code change.

Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: no known author-side action remains for this template-body repair after this update; maintainer or bot re-evaluation may still be needed.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: this body update addresses the current-template PR body gap by replacing the older expanded template with the current top-level sections: Summary, Linked context, Real behavior proof, Tests and validation, Risk checklist, and Current review state.

target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left

target.title: fix(reply): defer context compaction safely
target.body:
## Summary

This extracts the context-compaction defer path from #85941 into a focused PR.

- defer context-engine-owned budget compaction to background maintenance instead of blocking reply startup
- keep successful compaction no-ops compatible with plugin-authored free-form reasons
- share the explicit deferred-compaction sentinel between the compaction queue and reply preflight handling
- add coverage for deferred maintenance, compaction hooks, no-op compatibility, and task-flow persistence
- keep the original contributor commit author: Keshav's Bot <keshavbotagent@gmail.com>

Split from #85941 so the compaction behavior and compatibility surface can be reviewed independently. Thanks @keshavbotagent for the original work.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...origin/codex/split-85941-compaction-defer`
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` on the grouped source branch before this split: 34 passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` on the grouped source branch before this split: clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reply preflight no longer aborts on successful plugin/context-engine compaction no-ops with free-form reasons, while the explicit deferred-compaction sentinel still fails the required preflight path.
Real environment tested: local source checkout on fresh `origin/main` split branch plus grouped source branch before extraction.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check origin/main...origin/codex/split-85941-compaction-defer`; grouped source proof ran `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` and autoreview.
Evidence after fix: split branch cherry-picked cleanly onto current `origin/main`; whitespace/conflict sanity passed; grouped source branch regression test passed and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: no diff-check errors; compaction no-op regression passed in the focused test before split; commit author preserved as Keshav's Bot <keshavbotagent@gmail.com>.
What was not tested: the full compaction test matrix and live context-engine/plugin compaction were not rerun after the split; CI should run the normal PR gates.

target.title: Add ClawHub skill verification and trust surfaces
target.body:
## Summary
- adds `openclaw skills verify <slug>` backed by ClawHub `/verify`, with `--version`, `--tag`, `--card`, shared/global workspace, and installed/uninstalled target resolution
- resolves installed ClawHub skills from installer-owned lock/origin metadata and fails closed on malformed trust envelopes/card downloads
- adds `skills.securityVerdicts` and `skills.skillCard` gateway methods so Control UI can show a top-level ClawHub verdict plus a lazy local `skill-card.md` tab
- keeps verdict fetching bulk/narrow: one passive unauthenticated request for linked installs, no card data and no full scanner payload; detailed reports link out to ClawHub

## Cross-repo dependency
- depends on ClawHub bulk verdict endpoint in openclaw/clawhub#2404: `POST /api/v1/skills/-/security-verdicts`
- deployed to production on May 26, 2026 and verified against `openclaw/discrawl@1.0.0`
- OpenClaw intentionally does not fall back to N per-skill requests; old/self-hosted ClawHub endpoints can report verdicts unavailable until the endpoint exists

## Tests
- `CI=true pnpm test src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.verify.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `CI=true pnpm check:changed`
- `CI=true pnpm format:docs:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/skills-status.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods-list.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/skills.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `pnpm protocol:check`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: installed ClawHub skills can show a compact Control UI trust verdict, lazy-load the local `skill-card.md`, and link to the full ClawHub security report without cloning the scanner UI into OpenClaw.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw workspace with `openclaw/discrawl@1.0.0` installed from production `https://clawhub.ai`; source gateway from this PR branch on loopback port `18879`; ClawHub production security-audit page.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Installed `discrawl` from ClawHub into the local main workspace.
- Started a source gateway from this PR branch with channels disabled on loopback port `18879`.
- Called `skills.status`, `skills.securityVerdicts`, and `skills.skillCard` through the gateway.
- Opened the Skills detail view in the Control UI, checked the verdict overview, opened the Skill Card tab, and opened the full ClawHub security report.
- After the latest ClawSweeper findings, ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "schema": "openclaw.skills.security-verdicts.v1",
  "items": [
    {
      "registry": "https://clawhub.ai",
      "ok": true,
      "decision": "pass",
      "requestedSlug": "discrawl",
      "requestedVersion": "1.0.0",
      "slug": "discrawl",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "displayName": "Discrawl",
      "publisherHandle": "openclaw",
      "skillUrl": "https://clawhub.ai/openclaw/discrawl",
      "securityAuditUrl": "https://clawhub.ai/openclaw/discrawl/security-audit?version=1.0.0",
      "securityStatus": "clean",
      "securityPassed": true
    }
  ]
}
```

```text
skills.status confirmed discrawl is linked to https://clawhub.ai, installedVersion 1.0.0, with local skillCard.present true.
skills.skillCard returned the local card for discrawl from skill-card.md.
Control UI rendered the Clean verdict overview, Full security report link, and Skill Card tab.
The ClawHub production report opened at https://clawhub.ai/openclaw/discrawl/security-audit?version=1.0.0.
```

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/patrickerichsen/Git/openclaw/openclaw

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  60 passed (60)
Command: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts
```

Observed result after fix: the happy path renders the current ClawHub verdict and local Skill Card; the UI verdict helper now only shows clean when `ok === true` and `decision === "pass"`; installed CLI verification now rejects origin/lock registry disagreement before contacting a registry.

What was not tested: the currently installed packaged beta gateway does not expose these new methods until this PR ships; after the latest review fixes I reran the targeted touched tests above, not the full changed-file gate.

## Review
- autoreview accepted findings fixed: verified card URL binding, missing card metadata, card byte cap, release-owned changelog removal, fail-closed malformed decisions, lockfile-corroborated installed origin metadata, method reachability, token-safe passive verdict fetches, lazy/symlink-safe local card reads, provenance install-dir/registry checks, non-blocking verdict refresh, and stale Skill Card cache guards
- final read-only subagent review: no blocking findings after fixes
- final autoreview remaining finding rejected as an expected cross-repo dependency on openclaw/clawhub#2404, verified in that ClawHub branch

<!-- agent-transcript:start -->
## Agent Transcript

<details>
<summary>Redacted Codex session transcript: Add ClawHub skill verification and trust surfaces</summary>

````text
source: [LOCAL_SESSION]
redaction: local paths, emails, phone-shaped strings, token-shaped strings, auth headers, auth query params
omitted: raw tool outputs, system/developer prompts, local paths, secrets, browser/session/auth details, unrelated turns
scope: selected PR-relevant turns only

[user]
Linked the earlier draft OpenClaw PR and the merged ClawHub Skill Card work, then asked to mirror the current ClawHub CLI behavior in OpenClaw so users can verify a skill.

[assistant]
Grounded the work in the newer ClawHub contract rather than the stale draft. Identified the OpenClaw work as a consumer-side implementation for ClawHub `/verify` plus Skill Card behavior, with installed-skill resolution from ClawHub origin metadata.

[user]
Asked whether ClawHub's hashing fixes for generated `skill-card.md` would affect OpenClaw.

[assistant]
Decided OpenClaw verification should not recompute local bundle hashes. The installed bundle may contain generated `skill-card.md`, but OpenClaw should resolve the installed slug/version from local provenance and trust ClawHub's verification envelope. Added a regression requirement so local generated cards do not cause source-fingerprint mismatch failures.


[user]
Clarified the CLI surface: mirror `--version`, `--tag`, and `--card`; remove `--json` because JSON is already the default.

[assistant]
Implemented the OpenClaw CLI work test-first. Added ClawHub client helpers for the current `/verify` and card fetch contracts, installed-origin target resolution, `openclaw skills verify`, JSON-default output, `--card`, failure exit behavior, and generated `skill-card.md` regression coverage.

[assistant]
During review, accepted and fixed trust-surface findings: `--card` must fetch the exact verified `card.url`, card downloads must be bounded, and successful verification must require both `ok === true` and `decision === "pass"`. Reran focused tests and changed-file gates after each fix.

[user]
Asked how the Control UI should show security scan info and the Skill Card, and whether OpenClaw should fetch full scan details from ClawHub at page load.

[assistant]
Separated the authority boundaries:
- disk state proves install provenance and can provide local `skill-card.md`;
- ClawHub remains the runtime authority for the current security verdict;
- ClawHub remains the UI owner for the full security report.

[user]
Asked whether OpenClaw would need `n` requests to refresh verdicts for many installed skills and whether a bulk endpoint was needed.

[assistant]
Recommended a new ClawHub bulk security verdict endpoint rather than per-skill verify calls or full scan fetches. The endpoint should return compact top-level verdicts for exact `{ slug, version }` pairs, and OpenClaw should render only the minimal verdict summary plus a link to the full ClawHub report.

[user]
Confirmed that OpenClaw should not fetch card data remotely because installed ClawHub skills already have local `skill-card.md`.

[assistant]
Updated the design accordingly: Control UI reads the Skill Card from disk, fetches only current security verdicts from ClawHub, avoids maintaining a duplicate scanner UI, and links out to ClawHub for details.

[assistant]
Implemented the OpenClaw UI/gateway side. Added ClawHub linkage and local Skill Card metadata to skills status, new gateway methods for bulk `skills.securityVerdicts` and lazy local `skills.skillCard`, protocol models, UI controller caching/loading, and Skills view rendering for verdict overview, full security report link, and the Skill Card tab.

[assistant]
Collected local proof with `openclaw/discrawl@1.0.0`: installed the skill, confirmed local `skill-card.md` and ClawHub origin metadata, verified the production bulk verdict returned `pass` / `clean`, and confirmed the source gateway rendered the verdict overview and Skill Card tab. Saved UI screenshots for PR proof.

[tool summary]
Local-only transcript render; raw tool outputs omitted. Relevant implementation proof included targeted Vitest shards, changed-file gates, autoreview fixes, protocol generation, and browser verification of the OpenClaw Skills UI against a locally running source gateway.
````
