target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 1 protocol foundation
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the narrow foundation. It creates the versioned broker SDK subpath and bundled channel-broker plugin, but it does not move Telegram, Discord, Slack, or any user defaults. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. - Head: `483c5b80e2` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `483c5b80e2` Focused local validation after the latest Phase 1 package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));' ``` - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above: ```json { "discoveredChannel": { "id": "channel-broker", "order": 60, "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker" }, "channelNormalization": "channel-broker", "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7", "outboundProtocolVersion": 1, "outboundMode": "final", "outboundPlatform": "telegram", "outboundConversation": { "id": "chat 123", "type": "channel", "threadId": "topic-7" }, "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof" } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw discovered the bundled `channel-broker` entry, normalized the channel id, built a Telegram broker target that preserved `threadId`, and emitted a V1 final outbound request with normalized `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.threadId: "topic-7"`, and payload text `broker proof`. - **What was not tested:** No real third-party broker provider delivery was tested in Phase 1. target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and trusted inbound peer candidates for the current automatic-delivery turn, including `identityLinks` aliases.
- Threads inbound peer context through automatic replies, routed replies, follow-up routed replies, durable turn replies, and durable outbound delivery.
- Keeps send-policy-only predicates out of shared allow/deny scopes.
- Preserves explicit-send compatibility by keeping explicit `message.send`, gateway sends, and message-tool sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.
- Persists `sendPolicyMode` for queued delivery replay and treats legacy queued entries without that field as explicit replay, preserving pre-PR queue semantics.
- Reports blocked automatic durable sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs/help labels for the new predicate.

Fixes #85175.

## Verification

Latest head: `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`

Focused recovery, deliver, and send-policy validation requested by ClawSweeper:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       110 passed (110)
```

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.20s
Tests 14 passed
```

Additional focused validation for the explicit-send footprint trim and durable reply callers:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 30.40s
Gateway shard: 51 passed
Infra shard: 52 passed
Agents shard: 59 passed
Channels shard: 55 passed
```

Type/lint checks:

```text
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue-recovery.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/skill-tool-dispatch.runtime.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/plugins/tool-types.ts
```

Result:

```text
pnpm tsgo:core passed
oxlint passed
```

## Footprint reduction

The PR surface was reduced from ClawSweeper's reviewed `43 files / +1185 -58` to:

```text
30 files changed, 1038 insertions(+), 58 deletions(-)
```

Removed from the PR footprint:

- explicit message-tool `inboundPeer` propagation
- plugin tool-context `inboundPeer` propagation
- message-action-runner explicit-send `inboundPeer` propagation
- outbound-send-service explicit-send `inboundPeer` propagation
- related tests whose only purpose was proving explicit paths carried `inboundPeer`

Reasoning: explicit sends intentionally bypass `session.sendPolicy`, so passing `inboundPeer` through those explicit paths was unnecessary surface area. The automatic/durable reply path remains covered.

## Legacy queue compatibility

Recovery now treats queued delivery entries without `sendPolicyMode` as explicit replay:

```text
sendPolicyMode: entry.sendPolicyMode ?? "explicit"
```

This preserves pre-existing queued `message.send` / gateway retry behavior after upgrade under `session.sendPolicy.default: "deny"`.

Regression added:

```text
src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts
- replays legacy queued entries without sendPolicyMode as explicit under deny-by-default policy
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` must allow same-peer automatic Telegram durable replies and suppress wrong-peer automatic durable replies before platform send. The same PR also preserves explicit-send compatibility for legacy queued entries without `sendPolicyMode`.

- **Real environment tested:** Live Telegram bot setup using OpenClaw PR-head source at proof time, with a real Telegram direct peer `TG_PEER_A` and a distinct redacted target `TG_PEER_B`. Latest live Telegram proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85763#issuecomment-4529388676. Current code-review head is `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`; the live proof head was `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** On current head `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`, I reran the focused recovery, deliver, send-policy, durable-reply, gateway-send, message-tool, typecheck, and lint commands listed in the Verification section. The live Telegram proof flow used the same durable `sendDurableMessageBatch` policy path: configure `session.sendPolicy` to deny direct Telegram when `{ "peerEquals": "inboundPeer", "invert": true }`, send one automatic durable reply to `TG_PEER_A`, then attempt an automatic durable send to `TG_PEER_B` in the same inbound-peer context.

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live Telegram proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85763#issuecomment-4529388676. Copied redacted runtime output from that proof:

```text
Inbound Telegram direct session: agent:main:telegram:direct:TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC
Inbound peer candidates: TG_PEER_A, TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC, TG_PEER_A_USERNAME

Same-peer allow:
  outbound target: TG_PEER_A
  durable result: sent
  Telegram message id: 6
  Telegram API calls for this case: 1

Wrong-peer suppression:
  attempted outbound target: TG_PEER_B
  durable result: suppressed
  suppression reason: denied_by_send_policy
  cancel reason: send_policy_peer_mismatch
  Telegram API calls for this case: 0
```

Redacted diagnostic excerpt:

```json
{
  "status": "suppressed",
  "reason": "denied_by_send_policy",
  "hookEffect": {
    "cancelReason": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
    "metadata": {
      "code": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
      "peerEquals": "inboundPeer",
      "expectedPeers": ["TG_PEER_A", "TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC", "TG_PEER_A_USERNAME"],
      "actualPeer": "TG_PEER_B"
    }
  },
  "telegramApiCallsForCase": 0
}
```

Required proof gate status on current PR body/head:

```text
$ gh pr checks 85763 --repo openclaw/openclaw
Real behavior proof  pass  15s  https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26369003419/job/77617646289

$ gh run view 26369003419 --repo openclaw/openclaw --log
External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Same-peer Telegram durable send to `TG_PEER_A` delivered with one Telegram API send call. Wrong-peer durable outbound attempt to `TG_PEER_B` was suppressed with `denied_by_send_policy` / `send_policy_peer_mismatch` and zero Telegram API send calls, so `TG_PEER_B` received no Telegram message. Current head additionally preserves legacy queued explicit sends by replaying missing `sendPolicyMode` as `explicit`, covered by `src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`.

- **What was not tested:** The real Telegram send itself was captured on proof head `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`, not repeated after the final `86f2586a` recovery/footprint commit. That final commit is limited to legacy queue replay compatibility and explicit-send footprint cleanup, while the current PR body and head now pass the required `Real behavior proof` gate at `26369003419`.

### Config under test

`session.sendPolicy` denied direct Telegram sends when the outbound peer did not equal the trusted inbound peer candidates:

```json
{
  "default": "allow",
  "rules": [
    {
      "action": "deny",
      "match": {
        "allOf": [
          { "channel": "telegram" },
          { "chatType": "direct" },
          { "peerEquals": "inboundPeer", "invert": true }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Proof verdict

- Same-peer Telegram durable send to `TG_PEER_A` was delivered.
- Wrong-peer durable outbound attempt to `TG_PEER_B` was suppressed by `session.sendPolicy`.
- Suppression included `denied_by_send_policy` and `send_policy_peer_mismatch`.
- The wrong-peer case recorded zero Telegram API send calls, so `TG_PEER_B` received no Telegram message.
- Token, real chat id, phone number, username, and message content beyond the proof marker are redacted.

target.title: fix(ui): ignore stale running rows after terminal chat
target.body:
## Summary
- keep a terminal current chat session from being revived by stale `sessions.list` or `sessions.changed` rows with `status: "running"`
- preserve real newly started runs by allowing newer `startedAt` rows after the previous terminal `endedAt`
- add regressions for stale list rows, stale websocket rows, and late stale websocket rows after the run-status toast clears
- include a lint-only cleanup for redundant `image-tool.ts` provider-registry casts required by current `pnpm check:changed`

Fixes #86939.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Webchat can flip back from `Done` to permanent `In progress` when a stale current-session row with `status: "running"` arrives after the terminal chat event has already cleared the local `chatRunId`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `27ff166db9d6`, Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, real controller/runtime modules executed via `node --import tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a current dashboard session row already terminal (`status: "done"`, `hasActiveRun: false`, `chatRunId: null`), applied a stale `sessions.changed` payload with `status: "running"` and `hasActiveRun: true`, then checked `hasAbortableSessionRun()`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the runtime command:

```text
environment=node v22.22.0 platform=linux
beforeAbortable=false
{
  "applied": true,
  "change": "updated"
}
{
  "key": "agent:main:dashboard",
  "kind": "direct",
  "updatedAt": 3,
  "hasActiveRun": false,
  "status": "done",
  "startedAt": 1,
  "endedAt": 2,
  "sessionId": "sess-main"
}
afterStaleEventAbortable=false
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The stale running update was applied for metadata freshness but normalized back to `hasActiveRun: false` and `status: "done"`, so abortable chat UI stays disabled after the terminal run-status toast clears.
- **What was not tested:** No live browser dashboard was driven manually; the proof uses the real session controller and chat abortability predicate, with UI/controller regression coverage below.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Note: running all three recommended UI test files in one `run-vitest` invocation timed out after 240s in this local environment with no failure output, but the three files passed when run individually.

target.title: fix(skills): sync plugin skills to sandbox workspace
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #86190 Plugin skills (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/`) are stored as symlinks pointing to real directories in installed packages. In sandbox mode, `syncSkillsToWorkspace` should copy skills into the sandbox workspace so the `read` tool can access them at `/workspace/skills/...`. However, `loadGeneratedPluginSkillRecords` did not populate `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` for plugin skills. As a result, sandboxed agents saw host-only symlink paths in `` that fail with: ``` Path escapes sandbox root: ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md ``` ## Root Cause `loadContainedSkillRecords` was called without setting `syncSourceDir` for plugin skills, so: - `skill.filePath` pointed to symlink path (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/...`) - `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` were undefined - `syncSkillsToWorkspace` couldn't copy the skill to sandbox ## Fix Create `setSyncSourceForPluginSkill` helper that sets only `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` without modifying `baseDir`/`filePath`. This: 1. Preserves symlink path as `baseDir` for display purposes 2. Sets `syncSourceDir` to the real directory path for syncing 3. Enables `syncSkillsToWorkspace` to copy from real path 4. Skills appear as real directories in `/workspace/skills/` ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Plugin skills under `~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/` (symlinks) were not synced to the sandbox workspace by `syncSkillsToWorkspace`. Sandboxed agents could not load plugin-provided skills because the `read` tool rejected host-only symlink paths with "Path escapes sandbox root". - **Real environment tested**: Windows 11 + WSL2 + Docker Desktop, Node.js v24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.22 built from source (commit 53bd8a5), Sandbox image: `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`, Sandbox mode: `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode=all`, Model: `tencent-coding-plan/glm-5` - **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: 1. Built sandbox Docker image: `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh` 2. Started gateway with sandbox mode: `node openclaw.mjs gateway` 3. Started TUI client: `node openclaw.mjs tui --local` 4. Sent request to trigger skill loading: "Use the browser-automation skill to explain its purpose" 5. Verified skills synced inside sandbox container: `docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 bash -c 'ls -la /workspace/skills/'` 6. Verified SKILL.md readable inside sandbox: `docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 bash -c 'head -30 /workspace/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md'` - **Evidence after fix**: **Screenshot 1 - Sandbox container running (Docker Desktop):** Docker Desktop showing `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim` container running. **Screenshot 2 - Plugin skills synced to /workspace/skills/:** Shows `browser-automation` (plugin skill from symlink) successfully synced as real directory alongside other skills like `weather`, `github`, `notion`, etc. **Screenshot 3 - SKILL.md readable from sandbox:** Shows the skill content successfully read from `/workspace/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md` inside the sandbox container - no "Path escapes sandbox root" error. - **Observed result after the fix**: - Sandbox container started successfully with `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim` image - 60+ plugin skills synced to `/workspace/skills/` as real directories (not symlinks) - SKILL.md files readable from inside sandbox container - Agent successfully loaded and explained the `browser-automation` skill - No "Path escapes sandbox root" errors in gateway logs - **What was not tested**: N/A - full end-to-end agent request completed successfully. ## Unit Test Evidence ``` npx vitest run src/agents/skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts ====================================================================== Issue #86190: Plugin skills unreadable in sandbox ====================================================================== [Step 1] Setup - Create real plugin skill directory Path: .../real-plugin-skill-2 Content: SKILL.md with name=wiki-maintainer [Step 2] Create symlink under ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/ Symlink: .../plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer Target: .../real-plugin-skill-2 (This is how OpenClaw exposes plugin skills) [Step 3] Create target sandbox workspace Path: .../target-1 (This becomes /workspace in the sandbox container) [Step 4] Run syncSkillsToWorkspace - Key fix: setSyncSourceForPluginSkill enables sync Sync completed successfully [Step 5] Verification Target skills/: wiki-maintainer wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md exists: true Directory type: real directory (FIX WORKS!) ====================================================================== TEST PASSED - Fix verified ====================================================================== Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 6 passed (6) ``` ## Test Plan - [x] New test: `skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts` with step-by-step verification - [x] Gateway startup with sandbox mode - no path escape errors - [x] Sandbox container running with skills synced - [x] SKILL.md readable from inside sandbox container - [x] All existing sync tests pass - [x] All existing plugin-skills tests pass

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities

- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
